source_item.title: Jio likely to begin IPO marketing from next week for India's largest offer

slide1_hook.label: India's Biggest IPO Starts Selling
slide1_hook.subhead: Jio starts talking to investors after strong Q1 results.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: Seven Years In The Making
slide2_backstory.body: Mukesh Ambani first talked about listing Jio in 2019, but the plan stalled for years. He finally committed to 2026, and Jio filed its draft IPO papers with SEBI on June 19.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Soft Marketing Begins Before Roadshows
slide3_news.body: Jio Platforms will start informal meetings with big investors next week, right after its Q1 results. The IPO could raise up to Rs 40,000 crore, with bankers valuing Jio between $100 billion and $180 billion.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Why It Could Shake Up Markets
bullets: Could beat Hyundai's IPO as India's biggest ever
Rs 27,500 crore of proceeds will cut telecom debt
Recent big IPOs give confidence in Jio's pricing

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Jio's IPO is finally here after seven years. Are you buying the hype, or waiting for the price band?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily breakdowns of India's biggest business stories.
